zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas ap Robert or Roger於大約1302年出生時，他的父親Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD32歲，母親Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION32歲。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas ap Robert or Roger于大约1302年出生时，他的父亲Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD32岁，母亲Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION32岁。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Rabbitt
meanings: English: from the Middle English male personal name Rabbode, Radboda (ancient Germanic Radbodo, from elements meaning ‘counsel’ + ‘messenger’).
English: from the personal name Robet or Robot, diminutives of Rob (see Robert ).
English: nickname from late Middle English rabet ‘young rabbit’. This word is not recorded until c. 1398, the usual Middle English word for ‘rabbit’ being cony (see Coney ).

variants.name: McRobbie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire): patronymic meaning ‘son of Robbie’, from the personal name Robbie, a Scottish pet form of Robert , with the Gaelic prefix mac ‘son of’.

variants.name: Rabjohn
meanings: English (Devon, Essex): from the Middle English personal name Robechon (of Norman French origin: Old Picard Robichon, Old French Robeçun), a double diminutive of Rob, a pet form of Robert , altered by folk etymology to Robjohn and Rabjohn.

variants.name: Nabb
meanings: English (Lancashire): topographic name for someone who lived by a nab, Middle English nabbe ‘hillock, knoll’ (Old Norse nabbi ‘projecting peak, hill’), or a habitational name from any of the many minor places in northern and eastern England named with this word, for example Whalley Nab in Blackburn (Lancashire), Nab Scar in Rydal (Westmorland), and The Nab in Burgh Saint Margaret (Norfolk).
English (Lancashire): alternatively, it may be from the Middle English personal names Nabb and Napp, which are probably parallels to Nobb and Nopp, pet forms of Robert . Compare Nobbs .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Aba(dh) ‘son of the abbot’, see McNab .

variants.name: Probert
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Roppert ‘son of Robert’(see Robert ).

variants.name: Rabb
meanings: Scottish and English (Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Rab(b), a pet form of Robert with unrounding of the first vowel. Compare Robb .
Hungarian: variant of   Rab  .

variants.name: Rapson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rab(b) (a pet form of Robert with unrounding of the first vowel) + -son, with devoicing of /b/ to /p/. Compare Robson and Rabb .

variants.name: McCobb
meanings: Irish: from Mac Hob, ‘son of Hob ‘, an English pet form of Robert . Compare Hobson .

variants.name: McRoberts
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Roibeirt, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of the personal name Robert , with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Robin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Emile, Marcel, Achille, Armand, Arnaud, Camille, Julien, Michel, Normand.
French, Breton, Scottish, English, and German: from the personal name Robin, a pet form of Robert , composed of the short form Rob + the hypocoristic suffix -in.
Slovenian: topographic name derived from rob ‘edge, border’ (see Robar ).

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philippe, Alcee, Armand, Laurent, Leonce, Marcel, Yves, Clovis, Fernand, Gilberte, Janvier, Jean Claude.

Scottish, English, North German, French, Walloon, Catalan, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements hrōd ‘renown’ +gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, which was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rog(i)er and was reinforced by the Old Norse cognate Hróthgeirr.

History: Gabriel Roger from Sainte-Verge in Deux-Sèvres, France, married Marie (de) Lacour in Quebec City, QC, in 1669. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Rogero
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Cayetano, Jose, Silva.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogero (see Roger ).
Catalan (Rogeró): from a pet form of the personal name Roger .

variants.name: Rieck
meanings: South German: from a pet form of the personal name Ru(o)diger, a compound of Old High German hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + gēr ‘spear, lance’ (see Roger ).
North German: from a short form of a personal name containing the element rīc ‘power(ful)’.
Germanized form of Sorbian Rjek: nickname from Upper Sorbian rjek ‘hero’. Compare Reck .

variants.name: Rogelio
meanings: Spanish; Portuguese (Rogélio): from the personal name Rogelio, Portuguese Rogélio, of ancient Germanic origin (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Roger  .

variants.name: Rogers
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Roger , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Ruaidhrí (see Rorie ).

History: Thomas Rogers (c. 1587–1621), born in London, England, was among the Pilgrim Fathers who sailed on the Mayflower in 1620. He died during the first winter at Plymouth Colony, but his son Joseph survived and married, and was later joined in MA by his brother John. This name was subsequently brought to North America independently by many different bearers.

variants.name: Rogel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Jose Luis, Juan, Marcos, Margarita, Wilfredo, Adilia, Alejandro, Ana Gladys, Ana Maria, Anacleto.
Spanish: from the personal name Rogel, an equivalent of French Roger .

Americanized form of Slovenian Rogelj: nickname or topographic name from rogelj ‘horn, point’, figuratively ‘tall young man’, as a topographic name probably denoting someone who lived e.g. on a horn-shaped spur of a hill. The surname in the form Rogel is found in Slovenia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Prosser
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Rhosier ‘son of Roger’ (see Roger and Rosser ).

variants.name: Rogerson
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Roger + -son.

variants.name: Ritger
meanings: Altered form of German and Dutch Rutger or Rütger, from the personal name Rudiger (see Roger ).

variants.name: Rogge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Bernhard, Elke, Hans, Helmut, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name   Roger  .
North German and Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a grower of rye or a baker of rye bread, from Low German, Dutch rogge ‘rye’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas ap Robert or Roger was born about 1302, his father, Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD, was 32 and his mother, Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION, was 32. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas ap Robert or Roger est né environ 1302, son père, Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas ap Robert or Roger um 1302 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD 32 und seine Mutter Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION 32. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas ap Robert or Roger è nato il circa 1302, il suo padre, Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION, aveva 32 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas ap Robert or Rogerが約1302年に生まれた時，父Robert " Roger" Ap EMERODは32歳，母Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLIONは32歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas ap Robert or Roger(이)가 대략 1302년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION(은)는 32세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas ap Robert or Roger nasceu aproximadamente 1302, seu pai, Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION, tinha 32 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas ap Robert or Roger родился около 1302 г., его отцу по имени Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION, 32. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas ap Robert or Roger nació aproximadamente 1302, su papá, Robert " Roger" Ap EMEROD, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Gwerful Ferch HYWEL CAERLLION, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
